runs.context_blueprint: {"language": "Go", "build_system": "go", "package_manager": "modules", "build_commands": ["go mod download", "CGO_ENABLED=0 go build -v -o mkcert"], "install_commands": [], "runtime_requirements": {"language_version": "1.18", "system_packages": []}, "repo_type": "cli_tool", "base_image": "golang:bookworm", "base_image_rationale": "Official Go image on Debian Bookworm providing a Go 1.18-compatible build environment", "pitfalls": ["Go version in golang:bookworm may not exactly match Go 1.18 from go.mod; this can cause minor compatibility differences.", "No explicit multi-stage reduction in this blueprint; final image may include build tooling unless step is added to copy a binary into a lean runtime image.", "Version embedding (main.Version) relies on -ldflags; ensure to set Version during release builds if needed."], "notes": "This blueprint targets building the mkcert CLI from source using Go modules. For a slimmer runtime image, consider a multi-stage Dockerfile that compiles in a golang image and copies the resulting mkcert binary into a minimal runtime image."}
runs.final_dockerfile:
# Multi-stage Dockerfile for mkcert

FROM golang:bookworm AS builder
WORKDIR /src
# Cache dependencies
COPY go.mod go.sum ./
RUN go mod download
COPY . .
# Build with CGO disabled for a static binary
RUN CGO_ENABLED=0 GOOS=linux GOARCH=amd64 go build -v -o mkcert

FROM debian:bookworm-slim
# Install CA certificates for TLS trust
RUN apt-get update && \
    apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends ca-certificates && \
    rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*
# Copy the pre-built binary from builder
COPY --from=builder /src/mkcert /usr/local/bin/mkcert
# Run as the binary by default
ENTRYPOINT ["mkcert"]

artifacts.content:
# Multi-stage Dockerfile for mkcert

FROM golang:bookworm AS builder
WORKDIR /src
# Cache dependencies
COPY go.mod go.sum ./
RUN go mod download
COPY . .
# Build with CGO disabled for a static binary
RUN CGO_ENABLED=0 GOOS=linux GOARCH=amd64 go build -v -o mkcert

FROM debian:bookworm-slim
# Install CA certificates for TLS trust
RUN apt-get update && \
    apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends ca-certificates && \
    rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*
# Copy the pre-built binary from builder
COPY --from=builder /src/mkcert /usr/local/bin/mkcert
# Run as the binary by default
ENTRYPOINT ["mkcert"]
